post.description: Verify credentials and issue a signed access token.

get.description: List the books the current user owns (the shelf), newest first.

schema.title: Response List Books Api Books Get

post.description:
Validate + store a PDF, create the book, and trigger Phase A ingest.

Returns the freshly-created book (status ``importing``) directly — the shelf
prepends it and polls until it is ``ready``.

Defends the ingest pipeline: the content-type is checked, the per-user book
quota is enforced, the body is read **streaming** (aborting the moment it
crosses ``MAX_PDF_BYTES`` rather than buffering an oversized payload), and the
page count is capped (``MAX_INGEST_PAGES``) before any token-spending ingest
is triggered.

get.description: Fetch a book with its import status + progress.
A page's presigned image URL, text, and per-word boxes (§9.4).

The book's canon graph: the entity list the Director editor renders, plus
the human-inspectable markdown vault export (§8.1).
The book's shots (the §5.4 shot timeline) as a bare array.

schema.title: Response List Shots Api Books  Book Id  Shots Get

post.description: Open a reading session against a book (Redis control state + a durable row).

get.description: Return the Scheduler's live control state for a session.

post.description: Apply a debounced reading-intent update and run one control tick (§4.9).
Jump to a word: cancel distant work, bridge keyframe, re-seed (§4.8).
Classify a Director region-comment, enqueue a regen, emit agent_activity (§5.4).
Edit a canon entity and surgically regen only the dependent shots (§8.7).
Record the Director's resolution of a surfaced conflict and announce it (§7.2).

get.description: SSE stream of this session's §5.6 generation events.

SSE stream of ingest progress for the signed-in user's library (§5.1).

The shelf subscribes here for live ``ingest_progress`` events instead of
polling alone. Events are published on the per-user library channel while
Phase A runs.

Recompute the §4.10 watermark sawtooth for a session (zero video-seconds).

Ownership is enforced against the durable session row; the live Scheduler
control state (Redis) supplies the current focus word + velocity when present,
otherwise the durable row's values are used.

schema.title: Response Get Buffer Trace Api Eval Buffer Trace  Session Id  Get

get.description:
Return the cached crew-vs-baseline §13 report for a book (exact contract).

The report is produced (and cached) by ``python -m app.eval.run --book
<book_id>``; running both arms on demand inside the request path is
deliberately avoided (it is expensive). A 404 with guidance is returned when
no report has been cached yet.

schema.title: Response Get Report Api Eval Report  Book Id  Get

BookResponse.title: BookResponse
BookResponse.description:
A book on the shelf, with its import status and progress (§5.1).

The collection endpoints (``GET /books``) return a *bare* ``list`` of these,
and the upload endpoint returns a bare ``BookResponse`` — the frontend client
parses ``Book[]`` / ``Book`` directly (no envelope).

BufferTracePoint.title: BufferTracePoint
BufferTracePoint.description: One sample on the committed-buffer sawtooth (the shared contract item).

CanonAppearance.title: CanonAppearance
CanonAppearance.description: An entity's appearance block with presigned reference-image URLs.

CanonEditRequest.title: CanonEditRequest
CanonEditRequest.description: An edit to a canon entity, triggering surgical dependent regen (§5.4/§8.7).

CanonEditResponse.title: CanonEditResponse
CanonEditResponse.description:
The new entity version + the dependent shots queued for regen (§8.7).

``affected_shot_ids`` are the dependent shots the edit re-rendered — the name
the frontend's canon editor reads to mark those shots "rendering".

CanonEntityResponse.title: CanonEntityResponse
CanonEntityResponse.description:
One canon entity (current version) projected for the §5.4 canon editor.

``id`` is the stable ``entity_key`` (what the canon-edit call targets), not
the per-version DB row id.

CanonReferenceImage.title: CanonReferenceImage
CanonReferenceImage.description: A locked reference image projected for the Director's canon editor.

CanonResponse.title: CanonResponse
CanonResponse.description:
The canon graph for a book: the entity list the Director editor renders,
plus the optional human-inspectable markdown vault export (§8.1).

CommentRequest.title: CommentRequest
CommentRequest.description: A Director region-comment: a screenshot + a natural-language note (§5.4).

CommentResponse.title: CommentResponse
CommentResponse.description: How a comment was routed and the regen it triggered (§5.4).

ConflictChoiceRequest.title: ConflictChoiceRequest
ConflictChoiceRequest.description: The Director's resolution of a surfaced conflict (§7.2).

ConflictChoiceResponse.title: ConflictChoiceResponse
ConflictChoiceResponse.description: Acknowledgement that a conflict choice was recorded (§7.2).

CreateSessionRequest.title: CreateSessionRequest
CreateSessionRequest.description: Open a reading session against a book.

IntentRequest.title: IntentRequest
IntentRequest.description: A debounced reading-intent update (§4.3): focus word ``w`` + velocity ``v``.

IntentResponse.title: IntentResponse
IntentResponse.description: What one control tick did (§4.9): promotions, keyframes, buffer state.

LoginRequest.title: LoginRequest
LoginRequest.description: Exchange credentials for an access token.

PageResponse.title: PageResponse
PageResponse.description: One rendered page: a presigned image URL, text, and per-word boxes (§9.4).

SeekRequest.title: SeekRequest
SeekRequest.description: A jump to a word (§4.8): cancel distant work, bridge, re-seed.

SessionResponse.title: SessionResponse
SessionResponse.description: The Scheduler's view of a reading session (§4.9).

ShotResponse.title: ShotResponse
ShotResponse.description:
A shot's episodic record projected for the timeline / Director tools.

Includes ``source_span`` so the client's SyncEngine can sort/seek by reading
position; the §5.4 timeline returns a *bare* ``list`` of these.

UserResponse.title: UserResponse
UserResponse.description: A public view of a user account.
